what_it_does: A channel-level loop-prep document where lead, DNA, fork, projection, and recollection each contribute criteria/requirements, capability inventories, things Tim has mentioned, and form intents; it synthesizes these into unified surface criteria and a payload of new scope items for the commission completion register.
observations: The synthesis opens with two headlines: the engines are built but have no face, and FACE-1 is one surface composed by four contributors, not four parallel builds.
It defines a unified-surface build model: data (recollection) → route (fork) → host (projection) → render (DNA).

It lists concrete surfaces: channel-view, session-drill + embedded CLI, transcript-viz + search, timeline/lanes, board-view, address-view, tool-face, decision-surface, and supervisor-as-loadable-brain for the RHM.

A 'NEW SCOPE → REGISTER' section captures items like the theorem as base, the cube instrument, swappable axes, control-density/expertise axis, two-tier form bar, gap-surfacing-to-channel, 'use the models' acceptance bar, concurrent fan-out recall/theorem mine, per-slot content budgets, and past-session consult.
Each lane's contribution includes criteria, capabilities (with ★ for under-exercised), Tim-mentioned things, form intent, and grounded capability-use notes.
The file is truncated partway through recollection's capability-use section.
standouts: The commission is judged to be majority surfacing, not engine-building, because recall/render/backend/host engines already run.
FACE-1 is framed as a single unified surface built by all four lanes together, with the surface as the build unit.
Tim's theorem is positioned as the literal base of the whole system, with a live multi-dimensional 'cube instrument' gated on Tim bridging 'dimension' ↔ 'k'.
The tool-face is corrected as gated on a per-tool form-meta layer, not merely a generic invoke-door swap.
The supervisor-as-loadable-brain is designed to plug into projection's existing `forkVBrain.attach` seam.
concerns: The file is very long, dense with jargon, and truncated before the recollection section ends.
Many build items are gated on unresolved Tim decisions or conceptual bridges (dimension↔k, operator-attribution model #1b).
Some claims of 'fully done' sit alongside unbuilt parts (e.g., #1b attribution token), creating ambiguity about verification status.
The heavy use of inline symbols and lane-specific shorthand may impede reading by anyone outside the channel.
notes: A dense, multi-author channel-level prep board that maps what already runs in the company to the surfaces that still need a face, with the theorem and a 3D cube instrument as the deepest unresolved foundation.
questions: What is the exact content of Tim's theorem and how does 'everything is a variable' translate into the resolver law?
What is the 'dimension ↔ k' bridge Tim must provide before the cube instrument can be built?
Which concrete routes/files implement the READ-APIs fork lists for channels, sessions, transcript-search, board, and address resolution?
How is the #1b genuine-operator-attribution token supposed to be proven and enforced?
What is the precise boundary between FACE-0 keystone and FACE-1 surfaces?
What are the 160 source images and what structured output does their extraction produce?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the stated purpose is a channel-level loop-prep synthesis of collective criteria/requirements for the commission, and the file contains exactly that — synthesis, unified surface criteria, new-scope register items, and per-member contributions.
apparent_themes: Surfacing existing engines rather than building new ones
Form is half and the hard half; anti-text-wall
Theorem as the foundational base/variable resolver
Use models end-to-end; do not hand-author around them
Two-tier quality bar: floor vs fidelity
intention_signals: It is trying to consolidate distributed knowledge across the channel into a single preparatory artifact that defines what the commission face must be, surfaces under-used capabilities, and aligns the four lanes on building per-surface rather than per-lane, while elevating Tim's theorem and a live multi-dimensional instrument as the conceptual spine.
novelty: A channel-level 'loop board' that treats loop-prep as a fractal collective activity with an explicit four-lane contribution format, a unified-surface build unit, and a synthesis that the engines are built but lack a face; it also uniquely positions an operator's personal theorem as the system's base and a live 3D cube instrument as a face element.
contribution: The unified-surface criteria and the enumerated list of surfaces needing rendering; the synthesis that existing engines need surfacing; the cross-lane capability inventory, especially under-used tools; the new-scope register items (theorem base, cube instrument, swappable axes, control-density axis, gap-surfacing mechanism, content budgets, two-tier form bar); and the per-lane data-shape contracts and form intents.
summary_for_embedding: CHANNEL-LOOP-BOARD.md is a channel-scale loop-prep document synthesizing contributions from lead, DNA, fork, projection, and recollection into unified surface criteria, a register of new scope items, and per-lane requirements, capabilities, and form intents for building the commission's face.
intention_for_embedding: It was written to create a collective, fractal loop-prep system at the channel level that aligns the four lanes on co-building each face surface and records what Tim has asked for that is not yet in the completion register.
